Ad Text:
Are you ready to play a critical role in CS Energy’s transition journey? As our Climate and Sustainability Reporting Lead, you’ll be part of the Energy Transition Team, sitting at the centre of our climate risk, Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) and sustainability reporting uplift. In this role, you will help equip the business with the insights, data integrity and reporting frameworks needed to navigate a rapidly evolving regulatory landscape and transition confidently toward a lower emissions future. This is a high impact role for a technical sustainability specialist who thrives on elevating reporting quality, improving emissions accuracy and supporting enterprise wide climate capability.

Working closely with the Head of Sustainability and stakeholders across the organisation, you will lead our ESG disclosure program, strengthen emissions reporting, support decarbonisation planning and ensure CS Energy continues to meet emerging AASB, Queensland Treasury and national climate disclosure expectations. If you’re passionate about translating complex sustainability data into clear, credible, decision ready insights, this could be your next career defining opportunity.

Duties include but are not limited to:

Leading the development and delivery of CS Energy’s ESG and sustainability reporting program, including GRI aligned double materiality assessments.

Monitoring and interpreting emerging sustainability frameworks and regulatory developments to ensure ongoing compliance.

Overseeing Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions measurement and reporting, aligned to GHG Protocol and evolving mandatory reporting requirements.

Driving continuous improvement of data collection systems, methodologies, controls and emissions reporting processes.

Coordinating organisation wide climate related risk assessments, both transition and physical and supporting integration into enterprise risk frameworks.

Supporting the development, analysis and tracking of emissions reduction targets, pathways and decarbonisation performance.

Identifying and assessing ESG risks and opportunities, ensuring robust governance and reporting mechanisms.

Delivering training, guidance and internal capability uplift in sustainability reporting and climate related literacy.

Collaborating across Governance, Risk, Operations, Asset Management and Corporate Communications to ensure consistent, transparent and high quality sustainability disclosures.

Preparing insights and information for Queensland Treasury, auditors, industry bodies and other external stakeholders.

About You

What’s in it for you

Be part of a high-performing team that values collaboration and continuous improvement.

Professional development opportunities, including exposure to training systems and compliance frameworks. 

Competitive remuneration and benefits, with a focus on work life balance. 


To be successful in this role you will have the following skills and experience: 

Relevant tertiary qualifications in environmental science, sustainability, engineering, energy, commerce, economics or related discipline.

Postgraduate qualifications in climate risk, sustainability, ESG reporting, data analytics or a related field are highly desirable.

Additional relevant certifications (e.g., GHG accounting, climate‑risk analysis, GRI sustainability reporting) are advantageous but not essential.

Reporting, including application of recognised methodologies such as the GHG Protocol.

Demonstrated experience delivering sustainability, ESG and or climate‑related reporting, preferably within an emissions‑intensive or complex operational environment.

Strong business acumen, analytical capability and communication skills, with demonstrated ability to interpret complex sustainability and climate‑related data and translate insights for diverse stakeholders.

Familiarity with sustainability and climate‑disclosure frameworks (e.g GRI, SASB, TCFD, AASB S2) and an understanding of relevant regulatory and compliance expectations in Queensland.

Proven ability to collaborate effectively across functions and influence stakeholders to support sustainability outcomes, uplift capability and embed improved reporting practices.

About Us

CS Energy is a proudly Queensland-owned and based energy company that provides power to some of our state’s biggest industries and employers. We generate and sell electricity, we are an energy retailer to commercial and industrial businesses, and we are investing in new energy assets including renewable hydrogen, solar and wind farms, and battery storage.  

Every voice is important to us  
 
CS Energy is proud to foster an environment that builds respect, promotes inclusiveness, celebrates diversity and embraces the unique skills and qualities of all our employees. Reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process will be supplied to those who require additional support. 

As part of the recruitment process, the successful applicant may be required to complete a number of background checks including; reference checks, pre-employment medical, national police checks, right to work eligibility, verification of qualifications, and bankruptcy checks.

Ad Text:
We help the world run betterAt SAP, we keep it simple: you bring your best to us, and we'll bring out the best in you. We're builders touching over 20 industries and 80% of global commerce, and we need your unique talents to help shape what's next. The work is challenging – but it matters. You'll find a place where you can be yourself, prioritize your wellbeing, and truly belong. What's in it for you? Constant learning, skill growth, great benefits, and a team that wants you to grow and succeed. 

Where you belong:

The SAP Data and AI Adoption Team is dedicated to driving customer adoption success and accelerating measurable business outcomes. We bring SAP’s most innovative data and AI frameworks and solutions to life by enabling customers to fully realize the value of their data through SAP Business Data Cloud and the broader Data and AI portfolio. By fostering collaboration, innovation, and a strong customer-centric mindset, we ensure that strategy turns into sustained adoption, tangible impact, and long-term value realization.


Role Overview

As a Principal Consultant, Business Data Strategy & Advisory, you’ll lead customer engagements to bring SAP’s transformative frameworks for Business data to life at SAP’s customers. Reporting to the General Manager of Data & AI Adoption, your role will focus on thought leadership, fostering collaboration, and achieving customer success.

Key responsibilities:

Develop innovative business data and analytics concepts, strategies, and solutions for our industrial clients in both national and international contexts
Advise on the implementation and optimization of reporting and planning solutions with SAP Business Data Cloud from requirements analysis and technical design to implementation
Transform and modernize existing reporting and planning systems and integrate Business AI with the needed high quality data
Take on program and project responsibility and support in presales activities
Deepen and expand customer relationships through competent consulting and solution-oriented approaches

What you bring:

Strategic Visionary and Trusted Advisor: Deep expertise in Data & Analytics and data platform architectures, concepts and business impacts, with a strategic view that aligns with SAP’s vision and cloud methodologies like RISE or Clean Core. Proven ability to align technology and business strategies for impactful customer outcomes
Experienced data & analytics transformation Leader: 10+ years of experience in Data & Analytics architecture and business transformation, with a track record of inspiring customers, driving collaboration, and delivering high-impact solutions. Proven track record of delivering large scale data & analytics programs / projects in the SAP environment
Customer-centric and Change-Oriented: Strong focus on customer success and extensive experience leading large-scale transformations. Skilled in change management to ensure digital adoption and measurable value for clients

Must-Have Requirements:

SAP data and analytics expertise: In-depth knowledge of SAP’s Data & AI portfolio (SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP BW) and the correlation with SAP Business AI, SAP RISE including Clean Core principles and SAP BTP.
Experience working with leading third-party data platforms such as Databricks and Snowflake, alongside hyperscaler ecosystems including Google Cloud Platform, AWS, and Microsoft Azure.
Deep technical expertise in integration architectures across hybrid SAP and non-SAP environments, with strong knowledge of Data Mesh and Data Fabric approaches, Big Data and Data Lake technologies, and OpenSource technologies, including the Apache ecosystem.
Data architecture change management: General knowledge about hybrid data & AI architectures, with proven success in guiding impactful transformations and leading change management initiatives to support adoption.
Strategic relationship building: Strong networking skills within SAP and externally, with the ability to build trusted relationships with leaders, including CIOs and CTOs
Ecosystem: Strong ecosystem engagement across the SAP Data and Analytics landscape including Development, Sales, and Enterprise Architecture as well as with external partners, hyperscalers, and third-party vendors to foster innovation, scale capabilities, drive customer impact, and maintain trusted feedback loops to inform the evolution of SAP products and concepts.
Cross industry expertise and exposure: cross industry expertise with a deep knowledge in at least one industry to build relevant Use Cases for Business data platforms

Nice-to-Have Requirements:

Innovation & AI expertise: Experience integrating AI and emerging technologies to drive transformation goals and enhance customer adoption
S/4HANA & business transformation expertise: S/4HANA and business transformation knowledge to identify optimization & innovation potentials in customer engagements
Industry certifications: Relevant certifications, such as SAP Certified Architect or others, with recognition as a thought leader in enterprise architecture
Advanced change management skills: Expertise in advanced change management practices, with a history of driving engagement and complex organizational change

What we offer:

This role provides a unique opportunity to shape SAP’s global architecture strategy. You will:

Impact SAP’s Future: Lead transformative projects that define SAP’s strategy and drive customer success
Collaborate with Industry Leaders: Work alongside global teams, SAP executives, and strategic customers on high-value transformations
Career Growth Opportunities: Enjoy a clear path to advancement with opportunities for specialization or leadership within SAP’s ecosystem
Inclusive Work Environment: Experience a flexible environment fostering continuous learning, professional development, and innovation

#DA26 #AI26

Bring out your bestSAP innovations help more than four hundred thousand customers worldwide work together more efficiently and use business insight more effectively. Originally known for leadership in enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, SAP has evolved to become a market leader in end-to-end business application software and related services for database, analytics, intelligent technologies, and experience management. Ad Text:
Principal ERP Functional Specialists - (Fixed Assets/Projects)
 

Multiple Permanent opportunities available  
$173,345.00  - $193,906.00 pa (plus 12% superannuation and annual leave loading)  
Opportunity to work from our modern Chatswood Office  
Salary packaging, up to 12 extra days off per year, fitness passport and more!  


Applications Close: 11:59pm, Monday 6th April 2026


We are looking for Principal ERP Functional Specialists across Oracle Fusion Fixed Assets, Projects, and Receivables, and Pathology Billing applications to join a high-performing team at the forefront of digital transformation in healthcare with eHealth NSW.  

NSW Health is transitioning to Oracle Fusion ERP, known internally as StaffLink ERP. The eHealth Corporate Applications team oversees all StaffLink ERP modules, where you will act as a leader, partnering with senior stakeholders to deliver funded projects, drive innovation, and enhance decision making and financial transparency across one of Australia’s largest health systems. 


Role 1: Principal ERP Functional Specialist – Fixed Assets 
The Fixed Assets Specialist is responsible for implementing and supporting Oracle Fusion Fixed Assets functionality across NSW Health. As a key technical and strategic leader, this role will drive optimisation initiatives within the Oracle ERP Finance platform to ensure solutions are aligned with organisational objectives and industry best practice.

The Principal ERP Specialist partners with the ERP Platform Owners and senior stakeholders to drive innovation, shape the ERP roadmap, and deliver solutions that improve financial transparency, decision-making, and overall business performance. 

In this role, you will:  

Lead strategic optimisations for Fixed Assets within Oracle ERP Finance.  
Act as an enterprise ERP lead, partnering with senior stakeholders to shape the ERP roadmap.  
Deliver funded projects and continuous improvement initiatives.  
Investigate, design, configure, test, and support Oracle ERP applications both Fusion Cloud and R12 environments. 



Role 2: Principal ERP Functional Specialist – Projects 
The Projects Specialist is responsible for implementing the suite of Project Applications and providing support and system enhancements to NSW Health's user base. You will be accountable for the delivery of strategic optimizations for the suite of Project applications in the Oracle ERP Finance platform. This role ensures that ERP applications are developed according to best practice, and in alignment with corporate objectives. 

In this role, you will:  

Drive enhancements for Oracle ERP Project applications.  
Collaborate across Finance and IT to deliver scalable solutions that support statewide operations.  
Provide leadership on ERP roadmap and innovation initiatives.  
Investigate, design, configure, test, and support Oracle ERP applications (StaffLink ERP and StaffLink R12). 


To be successful in role 1 or role 2, we are looking for passionate leaders with the ability to understand complex information including:

Understanding of the core Finance Processes with hands-on experience of Fixed Assets or Oracle Projects        
Extensive experience in Oracle ERP functional roles (Cloud) with R12 grounding.
Experience integrating data between Oracle modules using API's, Invoice Import, WebADI, FBDI, and More4Apps loaders.  
Ability to enforce governance standards and oversee ERP design in large enterprises.
Knowledge of Fusion ERP OCI, ATP, APIs, and integrations is a bonus. 

These roles are internally referred to as Principal Digital Specialist – ERP 
 

How to apply
Share with us your cover letter and most up to date resume (up to 5 pages), highlighting your relevant skills and experience in the key business area/s required for the role.

We recognise that AI tools are increasingly being used to assist with resumes and applications. While we accept their use, we’re eager to see your personality and get to know the person behind the application!  

For further discussions around the role or recruitment process, including adjustments, please contact our Talent Advisor at Olivia.liu@health.nsw.gov.au or Hiring Manager Anthony Robidashvily at Anthony.Robidashvily@health.nsw.gov.au.

Manager Performance and Innovation (Chief Audit Executive)

Liverpool City Council | Office of the CEO | Permanent Full-Time
Growth doesn't happen by accident.

Liverpool City Council is building something different — an Office of the CEO designed to activate opportunity, drive revenue generation and optimise our property portfolio.

As the capital of South West Sydney, home to over 250,000 people and projected to reach 352,000 by 2046, Liverpool is forging its reputation as a globally connected city, supercharged by the new Western Sydney International Airport. One of the state's oldest local government areas and one of its newest cities, Liverpool is a shining example of transformation, sustainability and renewal.
This is the moment. We need the right leader to help shape it.

The Opportunity
Reporting directly to the CEO, this is a high-trust leadership role at the intersection of performance, innovation, risk and accountability. You will own Liverpool's performance monitoring framework, lead our Innovation Program, serve as Chief Audit Executive, and co-develop our AI strategy — reimagining how local government delivers for its community.

You will develop and embed a council-wide performance monitoring and reporting framework, lead an Innovation Program that drives optimal performance and breaks down silos, and oversee internal audit and risk compliance. Working closely with the Audit Risk and Improvement Committee, you will also co-develop and implement Council's AI strategy to transform customer service and service delivery, while delivering complex, multi-stakeholder projects on time and on budget.

About You
You bring a relevant degree in business, risk management, IT or a related field, combined with senior management experience and proven excellence in multi-agency project management. You have a track record of leading teams and fostering cultures of innovation and high performance, a sound understanding of local government governance and risk frameworks, and exceptional communication and stakeholder engagement skills. A strong commercial acumen and the ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-paced environment round out your profile.

You possess highly developed analytical skills and a demonstrated ability to rapidly establish performance metrics and KPIs that are meaningful at every level of the business — from executive leadership through to frontline teams. Your proven excellence in transforming complex data into actionable insights, including through visualisation tools such as Power BI and the development of dynamic dashboards, will be critical to success in this role. A strong commercial acumen and the ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-paced environment round out your profile.

Working with us - Systems Planning & Analysis (SPA) Australia

SPA Australia is a leading provider of integrated consulting, legal, commercial and analytics services to government and large organisations. Our multidisciplinary approach combined with trusted advisory and leading-edge technology and analysis takes our clients further. For more than five decades, Systems Planning & Analysis (SPA) has supported clients across the world to meet complex national security challenges. 

In 2025 SPA acquired Proximity Advisory Services, a well-established and highly regarded consultancy organisation with unrivalled legal abilities, exceptional commercial capability, and a raft of specialisations in consulting. Today, SPA has a global footprint, including more than 130 employees based across Canberra, Melbourne, Sydney and Adelaide.  

SPA Australia’s Defence & Analytics team provides senior decision-makers within the Australian Department of Defence (Defence) with timely, objective assessments that inform capability employment and investment strategies. 

Our team of former military professionals and operations research analysts integrates deep operational expertise with advanced analytic methodologies—including force generation modelling, agent-based tactical simulations, and operational mission analyses—to deliver trusted decision support that directly contributes to national security outcomes. 

SPA Australia offers decision-makers within the Australian Defence Force evidence-based insights that address capacity and capability gaps, strengthen operational effectiveness, and optimise the deployment of current, and future Defence capabilities. 

SPA seeks a passionate and dedicated Director to join our Defence & Analytics practice. This senior role provides strategic leadership to high-performance, cross-functional teams conducting advanced quantitative and qualitative analyses of Australian and adversary military operations and capabilities across all warfighting domains, missions, and functional areas. 

The Director will lead multiple concurrent workstreams—often under tight timelines—while rapidly assimilating new information, technologies, and concepts. The role requires the ability to anticipate emerging analytical needs, balance immediate customer priorities, and consistently deliver rigorous, defensible insights that inform senior decision-makers at strategic and operational levels. 

This role will suit an experienced defence professional with demonstrated fluency across priority military operations and the enabling capabilities that underpin them. The successful candidate will also play a critical role in business development, leveraging existing networks to identify opportunities, strengthen client relationships, and contribute to the growth of the Defence & Analytics practice.

Key Responsibilities: 

Provide strategic leadership to cross-functional teams conducting complex analysis across all warfighting domains assessing Australian and adversary military operations, capabilities and capacities. 

Foster a mission-focused, high-performance culture and provide strong leadership to project teams. 

Demonstrate and apply deep expertise in military operations and support and enabling capability roadmaps 

Build high level relationships with senior Defence stakeholders and be a trusted advisor. 

Support Defence clients in the planning, design, and execution of experimentation and operational analysis aligned with joint force design and integration priorities.  

Facilitate stakeholder engagements, including workshops and demonstrations. 

Oversee multiple concurrent analytical workstreams, ensuring timely delivery of rigorous, defensible analysis that supports strategic and operational decision-making. 

Write capability analysis reports and briefs and present results to stakeholders from a wide range of Defence organisation and varying levels of domain expertise 

Support clients in implementing operational and tactical plans that contribute to national security objectives. 

Identify strategic growth opportunities across the joint domain and lead growth within the defence joint operations market.  

Required Experience: 

Must be an Australian citizen

15 + years relevant Australia Defence and Australia Federal Government experience in military operations and associated technologies

Demonstrated expertise in providing one or more warfighting domains, missions, or functional areas to the Australia Defence Force

Background in capability development, systems engineering, operations or mission analysis

Proven ability to conduct rigorous research and analysis and form defensible conclusions using available, often incomplete, information

Strong written and verbal communication skills, producing compelling and clear analytic products and briefings to support senior-leadership decision-making

Experience leading small teams and projects in support of Australian Defence capabilities, or Australia Federal Government key projects

Demonstrated ability to apply innovative analytical concepts, quantitative tools, and data visualisation methods to military problem sets and present results to executive-level leaders

Experience in Australian military operational planning within an operational headquarters or in Australian Defence capability management (including requirements definition, capability documentation, modelling and simulation, or operations research)

International Defence cooperation initiatives experience highly regarded

Current Negative Vetting 2 (NV2) Australian security clearance, or willingness and ability to obtain and maintain one

Desired Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in a STEM discipline; or substantial military experience plus an advanced non-technical degree and proven Defence capability acumen (e.g., completion of the Capability Technology Management Program or equivalent) 

Current Positive Vetting (PV) Australian security clearance

Note: International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) and/or Export Administration Regulations (EAR) are applicable to this role and as such, the position will or is likely to require access to material controlled by ITAR and EAR (Controlled Material).  Therefore, any person occupying the position must be able to satisfy ITAR and EAR controls, which may require specific authorisation to access Controlled Materials.  Accordingly, your citizenship, previous citizenships, race or nationality, or substantive contacts where such contacts are affiliated with proscribed countries under ITAR may be a factor in determining your suitability for this role. Further information on this requirement can be found at: https://spa.com/about/compliance-statements/. If you are concerned about whether you will satisfy the requirements of the role insofar as they relate to ITAR and EAR, you should contact Talent@spa.com, who will be able to provide you with relevant information.

This role offers you the opportunity to work within a supportive and inclusive environment, where your expertise in credit assessment will be valued and nurtured. You will play a pivotal part in evaluating complex loan applications, collaborating with colleagues across departments, and ensuring customer service standards are consistently exceeded.

With access to ongoing training opportunities and flexible working arrangements, this position is ideal for someone who thrives in a communal setting and is committed to sharing knowledge and supporting others. If you are looking for a role that values your interpersonal skills, attention to detail, and commitment to excellence, this is the perfect opportunity to advance your career.

Join a well-established non-bank lender with a significant presence in the Australian market, offering stability and growth opportunities for your professional development.
Enjoy flexible working arrangements and continuous training opportunities designed to enhance your skills and support your career progression within a collaborative team environment.
Be part of an inclusive workplace where your contributions are recognised, and you have the chance to make a meaningful impact on both customers and colleagues through dependable credit assessment and exceptional customer service.

What you'll do:

As a Senior Credit Analyst based in Sydney, you will be entrusted with the responsibility of evaluating complex mortgage loan applications while upholding rigorous standards of customer service. Your day-to-day activities will involve collaborating closely with various internal teams such as Treasury, Settlements, Collections, Finance, as well as external stakeholders including brokers and mortgage managers. You will ensure that every application is processed efficiently within contractual deadlines while maintaining transparency in communication regarding credit decisions. By supporting less experienced colleagues and sharing your expertise generously, you will contribute to an inclusive team culture that prioritises dependability and mutual support. Your ability to navigate intricate credit scenarios-such as self-employed income assessments or construction lending-will be instrumental in recommending solutions that benefit both customers and the organisation. Success in this role requires not only technical proficiency but also a nurturing approach towards colleagues and customers alike.

Assess credit applications thoroughly in accordance with established Service Level Agreements, ensuring all decisions align with company credit policies.
Process loans efficiently to meet contract deadlines while maintaining high standards of accuracy and compliance throughout each stage of the application process.
Liaise professionally with staff, brokers, mortgage managers, originators, agencies, third-party suppliers, and applicants from acceptance through to settlement, fostering positive relationships.
Communicate credit decisions clearly to all relevant parties, ensuring transparency and understanding regarding requirements and outcomes.
Support other processors, underwriters, and members of the credit department as needed by sharing knowledge and providing guidance.
Maintain a strong focus on enhancing customer service skills and knowledge within the team, contributing to a pleasant experience for community partners.
Demonstrate commercial acumen by assessing credit situations that fall outside stipulated criteria and making thoughtful recommendations to Delegated Lending Authority holders.
Follow up on loan enquiries throughout the application process, ensuring timely responses and a high degree of customer satisfaction.
Evaluate, assess, and process loan applications according to agreed customer Service Level Agreements while upholding company values.
Meet yearly goals and targets as outlined in annual performance evaluations by consistently delivering reliable results.

What you bring:

Your background as a Senior Credit Analyst equips you with deep expertise in evaluating mortgage loan applications across varied scenarios-from standard residential loans to more complex structures involving SMSFs or construction projects. You bring proven experience from non-bank financial institutions where adaptability was essential due to rapid business growth. Your qualifications demonstrate dedication to ongoing learning; holding relevant certificates ensures you remain knowledgeable about evolving industry practices. In addition to technical skills such as assessing LMI eligibility or interpreting QS reports, your interpersonal abilities allow you to foster harmonious relationships both internally among colleagues and externally with clients. You are dependable when acting as a referral point for less experienced officers-sharing insights generously-and sensitive when resolving conflicts or addressing customer concerns. Your commitment extends beyond individual tasks; you actively contribute towards achieving collective goals by supporting others' growth through training opportunities.

Proven experience of at least three years in a credit assessment role within financial services or mortgage lending environments.
Current or previous holder of Delegated Lending Authority for mortgage loans demonstrating trustworthiness and reliability.
Holder of Certificate IV in Credit Management or Certificate IV in Finance and Mortgage Broking showcasing commitment to professional development.
Comprehensive understanding of the mortgage market as well as broader financial services industry trends.
Experience working for a Non-Bank Financial Institution (NBFI), ideally within entrepreneurial or fast-growing business settings.
Ability to assess complex trust structures or self-managed super fund (SMSF) lending scenarios with sensitivity to client needs.
Expertise in self-employed income assessment processes ensuring accurate evaluation of diverse applicant profiles.
Familiarity with Lenders Mortgage Insurance (LMI) eligibility criteria from key providers reflecting thorough industry knowledge.
Experience with construction lending including reviewing Quantity Surveyor reports is highly desirable for handling specialised cases.
Excellent interpersonal skills enabling effective collaboration within teams as well as considerate communication with clients.

What sets this company apart:

This organisation stands out as one of Australia's most respected non-bank lenders with an impressive portfolio exceeding $16 billion. Their commitment to inclusivity is reflected in their diverse workforce spanning multiple countries-including Australia, the Philippines, and Malaysia-creating an environment where everyone's voice is valued. Employees benefit from flexible working arrangements that accommodate personal needs alongside professional responsibilities. The company invests heavily in training opportunities so staff can continually expand their knowledge base while advancing their careers. Supportive leadership fosters communal success rather than individual competition; achievements are celebrated collectively which encourages loyalty among team members. The organisation's focus on customer service excellence means employees are empowered to make meaningful contributions that positively impact both clients' lives and their own professional journeys. Working here means joining a network dedicated not just to business success but also personal fulfilment through shared values of dependability, empathy, and collaboration.

Grant Thornton Australia is one of the world's leading independently-owned and managed accounting, advisory and consulting firms.

Our culture is underpinned by a commitment to our clients, people and communities, and our promise to ‘Reach for Remarkable’ by providing proven expertise, principled execution, and personalised experience.

In Australia, we’re proud to support the careers of over 1,500 remarkable people. With six offices nationally and partner firms in more than 145 markets, wherever you want to go, we can take you there.

The opportunity

As part of Grant Thornton’s national Sustainability Reporting Advisory team, you will play a senior leadership role at the forefront of sustainability reporting and regulatory change in Australia and globally. You will lead complex client engagements, support the development of high performing teams and contribute to the continued growth and maturity of the practice as organisations respond to rapidly evolving sustainability reporting expectations.

The Sustainability Reporting Advisory team is on a strong growth trajectory, driven by mandatory sustainability and climate related financial disclosures, heightened board and executive focus, and increasing integration of sustainability considerations into financial, risk and strategic decision-making.

We support organisations to apply sustainability reporting standards and regulatory requirements – including AASB S1 and S2, IFRS Sustainability Disclosure Standards and related frameworks – in a way that is practical, proportionate and aligned to business strategy and value creation. Operating as a truly national team, we collaborate closely with assurance, tax and advisory specialists to deliver integrated, commercially focused sustainability reporting solutions.

Our work spans the full maturity spectrum, from early-stage readiness and executive education through to first time and ongoing reporting, helping clients embed sustainability into governance, financial reporting, risk management and performance frameworks.

With mandatory climate related financial disclosures now legislated in Australia, the team plays a critical role in technical leadership, capability building and market education. We support clients as they prepare to report, while also strengthening sustainability reporting capability across Grant Thornton through internal technical leadership and collaboration with assurance and other service lines.

Senior Manager value proposition

As a Senior Manager in Sustainability Reporting Advisory, you bring depth, judgement and calm leadership to complex sustainability reporting engagements at a time of significant regulatory change. You combine strong technical capability with proven delivery leadership, enabling clients to navigate new reporting obligations with confidence while building sustainable internal capability. You play a critical role in leading teams, managing managers and setting the standard for quality, consistency and professionalism across the practice, and bring the credibility to support—and, where of interest, increasingly lead—client pursuits that strengthen trusted relationships and contribute to the firm’s growth.

Your role

As a Senior Manager, you will be a leader within the Sustainability Reporting Advisory practice. You will be responsible for the end-to-end leadership of significant client engagements, the management and development of managers and senior team members, and active contribution to the delivery excellence and strategic direction of the practice.

You will work closely with Partners and Directors to build and maintain strong client relationships, support business development initiatives and lead the development of high-quality proposals. This includes shaping client propositions, contributing to scope and approach development, and supporting the effective transition from proposal to delivery.

In addition to client delivery, you will play an important role in shaping service offerings, strengthening internal capability and supporting go to market initiatives. This role requires a confident, mature and experienced professional who is comfortable operating at executive and board level, managing senior teams and delivering high quality outcomes in a dynamic and evolving regulatory environment.

Key responsibilities

Client leadership and delivery

Lead the end-to-end delivery of complex sustainability reporting advisory engagements, with primary accountability for technical quality, delivery outcomes and client experience.

Act as a trusted adviser to senior management and executive stakeholders on sustainability reporting obligations, readiness and implementation challenges.

Oversee engagement planning, resourcing and delivery across multiple concurrent engagements, ensuring work is delivered efficiently and to Grant Thornton’s quality standards.

Provide senior technical direction on the interpretation and application of sustainability reporting standards, legislation and regulatory guidance to client specific circumstances.

People leadership – managing the managers

Lead, coach and develop Managers and Senior Associates, setting clear expectations and supporting performance, capability development and progression.

Build depth and resilience in the team by developing future leaders and strengthening technical, client facing and delivery capability.

Foster a strong, inclusive and collaborative team culture aligned to Grant Thornton’s national operating model and values.

Practice contribution and commercial support

Support business development activities primarily through high quality delivery, trusted client relationships and technical credibility.

Contribute to proposal development, including shaping scope, approach and deliverables, in collaboration with Partners and Directors.

Assist with engagement scoping, pricing inputs and transition from proposal to delivery to support clarity, efficiency and execution discipline.

Contribute to the refinement of sustainability reporting service offerings and internal delivery approaches based on practical client experience.

Technical leadership and market profile

Provide senior review and oversight of client deliverables, ensuring outputs meet regulatory, governance and assurance expectations.

Contribute to internal training, technical guidance and capability building initiatives across the firm.

About you

You have:

Deep experience interpreting and applying sustainability reporting requirements, supported by a strong foundation in financial reporting, accounting and assurance concepts.

A proven ability to translate complex regulatory and technical requirements into clear, pragmatic advice for senior executives and boards.

Significant experience in a professional services or advisory environment, leading client engagements and managing senior team members.

Strong delivery and commercial judgement, with experience overseeing engagement economics and client relationships.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the confidence and presence to engage credibly with senior stakeholders.

You are:

A confident, mature and experienced professional who brings sound judgement, perspective and calm leadership to complex situations.

Client focused and outcomes driven, with a strong sense of accountability for quality and delivery.

Comfortable managing competing priorities across multiple engagements while maintaining standards and momentum.

A collaborative leader who builds trust, develops others and contributes positively to a national, multidisciplinary team.

Motivated by building capability – in clients, in teams and across the firm – in a rapidly evolving sustainability reporting landscape.

Qualifications and experience

CA or CPA qualified, or tertiary qualifications in a related discipline.

Significant experience in financial audit, accounting advisory, sustainability reporting, ESG or sustainability assurance and advisory.

Strong knowledge of IFRS and AASB standards, with demonstrated capability to lead others in technical application.

Strong knowledge of Australian Sustainability Reporting Standards (AASB S1 & S2) and the Greenhouse Gas Protocol, with demonstrated capability to lead others in technical application.

Knowledge of other sustainability reporting frameworks such as IFRS Sustainability Disclosure Standards, TCFD, ESRS, or GRI is highly regarded.

Experience working with assurance teams and an understanding of audit and assurance processes is advantageous.

Career development

Grant Thornton offers a clear pathway to Director and Partner for high performing Senior Managers. You will be supported through leadership development, coaching, national and global networks, and opportunities to shape the future of the Sustainability Reporting Advisory practice.

Location

This is a national role. Applications are open to candidates based in Sydney, Melbourne or Brisbane.

About the role:

We’re looking for a Senior Data Scientist to join our Advanced Analytics Centre of Excellence. In this role, you’ll build, deploy and scale machine learning and experimentation solutions that directly shape loyalty strategy, customer decisioning and personalisation, partner performance, and customer experience across the Velocity ecosystem.

You’ll work in a cross functional environment alongside Insight Analysts, Analytics Engineers and business stakeholders, leading complex problem spaces end to end from framing and experiment design through to production deployment and measurable business impact, embedding models into production systems that power large scale customer and member decisioning.

You’ll report to the Manager, Personalisation Analytics and Data Science and join a team with established models in production, including recommender systems, propensity and regression models, supported by an evolving MLOps framework built on Databricks and AutoML. These models are actively used to power large scale customer and member decisioning across the Velocity program.

You’ll play a key role in shaping and owning the data science roadmap, working closely with the manager to prioritise initiatives and ensure delivery against commitments.

As Velocity accelerates its AI transformation journey, you’ll partner closely with our AI teams to identify, develop and embed AI driven capabilities into scalable products and decisioning frameworks.

 

In this role you will:

Drive the data science roadmap for your domain, identifying and prioritising high impact opportunities aligned to business objectives and ensuring delivery against agreed commitments
Design, build and deploy statistical and machine learning models using Python and Spark within a modern, scalable data and ML platform, including Databricks, with a focus on deploying models into production environments supporting batch and increasingly near real time decisioning
Lead the end to end lifecycle of models, from problem framing and feature engineering through to deployment, monitoring and optimisation
Develop and enhance recommender systems, propensity models and predictive models that drive commercial outcomes
Define and evolve experimentation frameworks (A/B and beyond) to inform decision making, measure causal impact and continuously optimise outcomes
Own the evolution of MLOps practices within data science, driving improvements in model deployment, monitoring and lifecycle management in partnership with engineering teams
Build scalable data products and reusable decisioning frameworks
Provide technical leadership and mentorship, raising the bar for data science across the team
Translate complex analyses into clear, actionable insights using effective data visualisation and storytelling for both technical and non technical audiences
Influence senior stakeholders and partner closely with analytics, engineering and business teams to embed data-driven decision making into workflows
Ensure all work aligns with Virgin Australia Group’s safety, security and resilience standards

 

You’ll be great in this role if you have:

A degree in a quantitative discipline such as Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics or Computer Science (PhD desirable)
Typically 5+ years of experience building and deploying machine learning models in a commercial environment
Proven experience leading end to end data science initiatives with measurable business impact
Proficiency in Python, with strong SQL skills and experience working with large and complex datasets (Spark desirable)
Experience working with modern data and ML platforms such as Databricks and/or AWS
A strong foundation in statistics, experimental design and causal inference
Experience shaping or working within MLOps frameworks and production ML environments
Strong communication and data visualisation skills, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders and drive data-driven decisions with measurable business impact
Demonstrated ability to operate in ambiguous environments and translate business problems into scalable data science solutions
Curiosity, ownership and a drive to automate and scale solutions
Experience in loyalty, travel or consumer products (nice to have)

 

Why this role is interesting:

Work on production grade machine learning systems that power customer decisioning at scale
Build on an existing ML and MLOps ecosystem with modern tooling (Databricks, AutoML)
Own and shape the data science roadmap and drive delivery of high impact initiatives
Help scale experimentation, personalisation and AI driven decisioning across Velocity

Job Title: CRM and Data Development Manager - Wesley Research Institute
Ad Text:
CRM and Data Development Manager – Wesley Research Institute

Lead and transform CRM and data strategy at Wesley Research Institute, using your expertise to directly fund life-changing medical research

Lead organisation-wide CRM strategy during a critical transformation phase
Own and optimise Raiser’s Edge NXT to directly enable fundraising success
Full-time | Flexible Remote Working
Impact of the organisation
Wesley Research Institute is a leading not-for-profit delivering impactful health and medical research outcomes. As the official research partner of UnitingCare Queensland (UCQ), WRI works with hospitals and services across Queensland to improve clinical care and community wellbeing.

From advancing treatments to improving patient outcomes, WRI’s work has a real and immediate impact on people’s lives. The organisation is purpose-driven, agile, collaborative and curious, with a strong focus on translating research into real-world outcomes.

As WRI continues to grow its fundraising capability, it is investing in the systems, data and insights that will power the next generation of medical breakthroughs. This role sits at the centre of that transformation.

Impact you will have in this role
Reporting to the Head of Philanthropy, you will take full ownership of WRI’s CRM and data environment; shaping how information is captured, structured and leveraged to drive fundraising outcomes.

This is a standalone role and a rare opportunity to build and evolve the engine behind fundraising performance. You will operate as the organisation’s CRM authority, partnering with leaders across fundraising, finance, research and clinical teams to embed data and insights into decision-making.

Balancing strategy with execution, you will design governance frameworks, build dashboards, uplift capability and solve complex challenges across the organisation. You will have the autonomy to identify opportunities for improvement and the trust to deliver them.

Key responsibilities

Own and lead the strategy, optimisation and administration of Raiser’s Edge NXT
Design and implement data governance frameworks to improve quality and consistency
Build reporting, dashboards and insights to inform fundraising strategy
Partner with stakeholders across fundraising, finance, research and clinical teams
Identify and implement improvements to systems, processes and data utilisation
Maintain high data quality through both system design and hands-on execution
Deliver training and uplift CRM capability across the organisation
Act as the internal expert and trusted advisor on CRM
Skills and experience

Tertiary qualification in Business, Information Systems, Data Analytics, or related
Experience in CRM strategy, optimisation and administration (Raiser’s Edge NXT highly regarded)
Background in fundraising within health, research or similar
Experience in Advancement Services, university fundraising, or similar data-driven fundraising roles is highly valued
Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate data into actionable insights
Experience improving data quality and implementing governance frameworks
Ability to operate both strategically and hands-on in a dynamic environment
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Experience delivering training and building organisational capability
High levels of initiative, ownership and accountability
Advanced Excel and reporting tools (Power BI or Tableau)
Understanding of privacy legislation and fundraising ethics
Driven to use CRM and data insights to accelerate funding for breakthrough medical research
This is an opportunity to take full ownership of CRM and data capability and play a pivotal role in enabling fundraising success and supporting medical breakthroughs that change lives.

Applications

Please apply by uploading your resume. 

Please direct all enquiries to Shonagh Dimmick at Impact Advising on shonagh@impactadvising.com.au

Job Title: Founder’s Office – Head of Strategy (For Entrepreneur / Philanthropist)
Ad Text:
Job Summary

A rare opportunity for an exceptional strategy professional to partner directly with a leading entrepreneur and philanthropist.

This is a high-trust, high-impact role focused on shaping and progressing the Founder’s most important strategic priorities. You will work side-by-side with the Founder to frame key questions, test ideas, and define areas of focus, before independently driving the thinking forward through rigorous analysis and implementation planning.

This is not a passive or purely advisory role. It requires someone who can combine first-principles thinking with independent execution, and who is comfortable operating with both ambiguity and accountability.




About the Client

This is a rare opportunity to work alongside a leading entrepreneur who oversees multiple organizations and initiatives and is now directing an increasing share of his time and resources towards philanthropic work aimed at creating a better Australia for all Australians.

Operating across research, partnerships, and public interest initiatives, the Founder manages a broad and evolving portfolio of responsibilities. This role sits at the centre of shaping how those efforts are prioritized, explored, and developed. A key focus is bringing business thinking, rigor and execution to a range of meaningful philanthropic endeavors.




About You

You bring 5-8 years’ experience in strategy, consulting, or high-performance operating roles, with a strong track record of solving complex problems and working closely with senior decision-makers.

You are highly analytical, intellectually curious, and comfortable operating with ambiguity. You are able to take an initial idea or question, structure it, test it, and develop a practical implementation plan.

You are known for your judgement, clarity of thinking, and ability to challenge constructively.




You demonstrate:

Exceptional problem-solving and analytical capability

Strong ability to structure ambiguous problems into clear workstream

Excellent written and verbal communication skills

Sound judgement and the ability to engage credibly with senior stakeholders

A high degree of ownership and intellectual honest

The ability to start from zero (an idea), imagine what is possible and develop the analysis and plan to make it a reality




You are:

Curious, rigorous, and independently motivated

Comfortable moving between big-picture thinking and detailed analysis

Low ego, high impact - focused on outcomes rather than appearing the smartest person the room

Adaptable and resilient, with a willingness to test, learn, and refine thinking




About the Role

You will work directly with the Founder to shape and progress a range of strategic initiatives.

This role is not a traditional “strategy planning” position. Instead, it is built around a cycle of collaboration, independent progression, and implementation planning. You will see your work come to life in the form of new initiatives which will contribute meaningfully to our nation.

Working closely with the Founder, you will initially brainstorm and prioritise a range of ideas together. You will then take ownership of progressing each priority - conducting analysis, developing hypotheses, actively seeking out contrarian perspectives and exploring solutions - before returning with clear insights and recommendations on next steps.




Responsibilities include:

Building a highly effective and trusted thinking partnership with the Founder

Progressing multiple complex strategic initiatives with clear and actionable next steps

Bringing back clear, distilled insights, including implications, risks, and opportunities

Supporting decision-making through high-quality briefs, memos, and strategic materials

Helping prioritize initiatives and ensure focus on the highest-impact areas

Working with stakeholders where required to ensure strategy translates into progress

Presenting program proposals to board




What Success Looks Like in 12 Months




After 12 months in the role, you will have:

Established a highly effective working rhythm with the Founder, enabling fast, high-quality strategic thinking

Progressed multiple strategic initiatives from early-stage ideas to active programs

Built a reputation for rigor, strong judgement, and practical thinking

Enabled better, faster decision-making through structured analysis and synthesis

Helped shape the direction of key philanthropic and public interest initiatives




Location

Currently Fitzroy, relocating to new office in Cremorne in late May.




Rewards

In return for your expertise and commitment, you will receive:

Competitive salary package

Five weeks’ annual leave

The opportunity to work in an inspiring and driven environment




What Next

If you're looking for a role where you can work at the intersection of ideas, analysis, and real-world impact, we’d love to hear from you.

Please submit your resume and cover letter outlining why this opportunity represents the right next step for you.

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

About Us

Pivotel delivers critical communication solutions across satellite and cellular networks - keeping businesses connected in the most challenging environments. We’re a technology-led organisation with a strong focus on innovation, scalability, and smart digital solutions that enable growth.

About the Role

This is a senior leadership opportunity where you will own and drive Pivotel’s Enterprise Information Systems function. You’ll take the lead in shaping our Microsoft ecosystem, ensuring our platforms are high-performing, secure, and aligned to business needs - while also enabling seamless customer and employee digital experiences.

If you enjoy sitting at the intersection of strategy, technology, and business impact, this role will give you the scope and autonomy to make it happen.

Key Responsibilities

Define and deliver the Enterprise Information Systems strategy and roadmap
Own and govern Microsoft platforms including Dynamics 365, Power Platform, Azure, and Entra ID
Act as IT Service Owner, accountable for platform performance, availability, and lifecycle
Drive a configuration-first approach, leveraging low-code and vendor-supported solutions
Lead the design and integration of customer-facing digital platforms and self-service capabilities
Ensure strong IT service management practices across incident, change, and release processes
Oversee risk, security, and compliance alignment across enterprise systems
Manage relationships with Microsoft, partners, and SaaS vendors, including licensing and cost optimisation
Provide clear reporting and insights to senior stakeholders on system performance and roadmap delivery
Enable data-driven decision-making through reporting, automation, and analytics capabilities

What You’ll Bring to This Role

Proven experience leading enterprise systems or IT service delivery functions
Strong expertise across Microsoft technologies, particularly Dynamics 365, Power Platform, and Azure
Experience operating as a service owner for business-critical platforms
Solid understanding of ITSM frameworks and governance-led delivery
Demonstrated ability to manage vendors, SaaS platforms, and enterprise roadmaps
Strong commercial acumen with a focus on value, performance, and scalability
A risk-aware mindset with experience in security and compliance environments
Excellent stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence at executive level
A balance of strategic thinking and hands-on execution

Why Join Pivotel?

Flexible working options including 2 days WFH per week, supporting balance and wellbeing.
Enjoy additional paid annual leave during your employment - 3 bonus days available from commencement of employment
Competitive salary with annual reviews to recognise your contributions and ensure fair progression.
Access to Employment Hero benefits, including discounts and cashback on everyday expenses like fuel, groceries, retail, and travel.
Employee Assistance Program (EAP) offering 24/7 confidential support for you and your family.
Supportive team culture - we value collaboration, continuous improvement, and celebrating wins together.

To Apply

If this role resonates with you and you are excited by the opportunity to lead and evolve a modern enterprise technology landscape, we would love to hear from you!

Your ability to shape strategy, drive platform excellence, and deliver impactful digital outcomes will be key to enabling Pivotel’s continued growth and innovation.

Pivotel is an equal opportunity employer and encourages applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people from diverse backgrounds, and those with disabilities.

All successful candidates will be required to complete an Australian Police Check prior to commencement.

Job Title: Retail Finance Manager, FP & A | Tapestry ANZ | Coach and Kate Spade
Ad Text:
About Us:

Make a Lasting Impression. Join Tapestry, the first New York-based house of modern luxury lifestyle brands. A successful career at Tapestry is built on hard work, determination, and a genuine passion for what you do. At Tapestry, you are part of a global house of brands such as Coach and Kate Spade that is built around our shared values of optimism, innovation, and inclusivity. We believe anyone from anywhere can have the best idea, and with creativity and perseverance, anything is possible. 

Primary Purpose:

Your will be responsible to analyse, report and reforecast sales, gross margins and related components and metrics on weekly, monthly, quarterly and within periods as needed on a timely basis through strong ability to synthesize data and liaising with key stakeholders across the multiple functions to ensure accurate results, robust assumptions and informed decision making. 

Skills and Attributes:

Drive the reporting, analysis and planning of sales and gross margin for ANZ direct businesses.
Track and communicate sales and business performance - synthesize and explain daily/weekly/monthly performance and variances against plans/targets/past results for regular review by local/regional/ leadership teams.
Identify and highlight SWOT, deliver key insights, and be a key contributor to crucial and timely discussion points.
Provide data points and insights to ensure appropriate merchandising and expense planning.
Support development of quarterly/annual forecasts and Long-Range Plans, while continuously aligning teams and plans to the strategies and direction.
Conduct ad-hoc financial analysis and reporting relevant to the covered markets / brands to support management decision making / fulfil requests from business partners.
 Manage and maintain all KPIs including but not limited to sales performance/benchmarking, traffic, conversion, productivity per store / size / customer/ labour hr, ADT, UPT, AUR, AUC, DR%, Gross Margin, IMU%. 

Who You Are:

5-8 years’ experience in management accounting, finance business partnering or commercial finance preferably in Retail or other B2C businesses
Bachelor’s degree or its equivalent in accounting / finance, merchandising, economics, or supply chain.
High intermediate to advanced Excel skills (transactional data retrieval and summarization including but not limited to vlookups, pivot tables, other data consolidation and summarization skills including sumif, sum product, and index/match, macro building a plus)
Experience in financial/merchandising/planning systems, business analytics tools (BI / PowerBI), and SAP a plus
Robust analytical skills united to a strong commercial mindset and the ability to create sound partnerships with key stakeholders
Ability to present and communicate with all levels including upper management
Ability to perform well under pressure with multiple deadlines, demonstrating a great deal of adaptability to a fast pace and flexibility managing change
Proactiveness as a self-starter and able to work both independently and as a team player.

Benefits and Perks:

Ongoing training and development opportunities within Tapestry ANZ
A supportive, inclusive, and collaborative team culture
Generous employee discounts across Coach and Kate Spade products!
Social events and much more!

You will be involved in a wide range of complex enterprise scale projects in transformation, integration, migration, application development, security, cloud and much more with our Tier 1 clients.

Type: Permanent, full time
Salary: Up to $170K package (including super), depending on experience!
Location: Melbourne
Start Date: Mid-April

Applicants must hold Australian Citizenship, Permanent Residency (PR) – DWS are unable to sponsor or support visas.

What you will bring:

Demonstrable IT Business Analyst experience as a Techno/Functional consultant operating in enterprise scale projects
Strong experience in Agile and Waterfall environments
A combination of demonstrated Technical AND Functional business focus in your work 
Exceptional communication skills both written and verbal
Essential, a formal BA qualification such as IIBA CCA, CBAP or AAC
Experience working on a broad range of projects with major businesses in different domains; financial services, government, professional services, retail, industrial, supply chain
Commercial experience in applying formal methodologies such as BABOK, Scrum, BPMN
You thrive in a constantly changing and dynamic work environments
Excellent people and stakeholder management skills
A proactive approach with client and a drive to exceed expectations
A flexible and adaptable attitude
Working at DWS offers you:

Ongoing professional and career development
Paid learning and certifications (chosen entirely by you), with completion bonuses
An active social club that host regular, subsidised events
Corporate discounts on a range of services and products, including health insurance, gym memberships, Apple products and much more
About Us:
Since 1991, DWS has been helping organisations across Australia reimagine what's possible through technology, business, and management consulting. With offices in Melbourne, Sydney, Adelaide, Brisbane, and Canberra, our 300+ locally based consultants bring deep expertise and fresh thinking to every project. We deliver end-to-end solutions spanning IT consulting, Staff Augmentation, Managed Delivery, Data & Analytics, Cloud, AI and Automation.
Our strength lies in our people; each consultant is carefully selected for their expertise, creativity, and commitment to excellence. Together, we deliver impactful solutions that drive innovation, efficiency, and long-term value. At DWS, we don't just meet expectations - we redefine them.

DWS encourages applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples.

Ready for a new challenge? Click APPLY now!
www.dws.com.au
  
No agencies please - our in-house talent acquisition team have this covered.

Job Title: Head of Digital Transformation - Adelaide based
Ad Text:
Polyaire is Australia's leading HVAC manufacturer and distributor, operating a complex, multi-site national network with a strong growth trajectory. We are seeking a senior transformation leader to take ownership of Polyaire's digital and enterprise systems capability.
This is not an IT leadership role. It is a commercially accountable enterprise position, responsible for defining, prioritising and delivering digital initiatives that drive measurable business outcomes.


The Role:

Reporting to the CFO, you will:

Own the enterprise digital architecture across ERP, CRM, BI, Supply Chain and eCommerce
Lead the prioritisation and sequencing of transformation initiatives at a portfolio level
Deliver end-to-end execution accountability, ensuring initiatives translate into measurable ROI
Drive operational performance across a high-volume, multi-site environment
Lead acquisition systems integration and scalable platform design
Apply AI and automation to improve decision-making, efficiency and commercial outcomes
Influence senior stakeholders across Finance, Operations, Sales and IT


The Candidate:

You are a commercially grounded transformation leader with:

Proven experience leading enterprise-scale digital transformation in complex, operational businesses
Deep understanding of ERP-led environments within distribution, manufacturing or B2B sectors
Demonstrated ability to deliver tangible business outcomes (margin, efficiency, working capital)
Practical experience implementing AI and automation in operational settings
Strong executive presence, with the ability to influence and drive change across the organisation


The Opportunity:

This is a rare opportunity to own and shape the digital backbone of a national business, with full accountability for delivering transformation that drives real commercial impact.

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples are encouraged to apply.

To apply please click apply or call Giuseppe Nasca on 08 8216 3508 for a confidential discussion.

Reporting to the Head of FP&A, this role is responsible for but not limited to the delivery of the monthly performance reporting, annual budgeting cycle and monthly rolling forecasts.


Working alongside other FP&A Analysts and the Head of FP&A, you will provide insightful reporting of business performance and play a key role in aligning business strategy and financial objectives.

Responsibilities

Monthly reporting and analysis - management reporting including Monthly Business Review and Board Pack preparation, insightful variance reporting and analysis, building and driving continuous improvement of financial reporting
Management of risks and opportunities and appraisal of impact on financial performance and budget
Business Partnering - supporting immediate business stakeholders, finance stakeholders and direct manager
Budgeting and Forecasting - build and maintain robust budget/forecast models, involving in annual budget cycles and monthly rolling forecasts

Requirements

5-7+ years’ experience, preferably with experience in the FMCG/ telco/tech industry
CA/CPA qualification
Very detail-oriented, proficient in financial modelling and analysis, and possesses excellent communication skills to effectively collaborate with cross-functional teams

Strong relationship building skills with experience in the effective management of diverse internal and external stakeholder groups
Advanced Excel skills.

What We Offer 

Working at Vocus will give you rewarding experiences and the opportunity to do extraordinary work. You will enjoy: 

Diverse and dynamic teams with a supportive and inclusive culture. 
Supportive career development plans with comprehensive ongoing training, support, and development opportunities. 
Generous discounts to over 400 retailers. 
Competitive leave options, including anniversary leave, purchased leave, parental leave, volunteering leave, study leave, and 5 extra days of Vocus leave each year, plus more.
Study assistance programs to excel your personal growth, learning and development. 
Health and wellness offerings, including access to our wellbeing initiatives that can help you from a financial, psychological, and physical perspective. 

Working at Vocus is never just a job – it’s personal. We’re crazy about our customers and believe our people are the difference. Our wonderfully diverse team and a vibrant culture define us. We care about the impact we have on our people, our customers, and our communities and are committed to operating as a responsible, ethical business. 

We pride ourselves on setting audacious and ambitious goals and believe that we can play our part in changing the Australian telecommunications landscape and make a fundamental difference to people’s lives. 

With us, you’ll have the opportunity to lead and inspire teams, work on projects that are shaping the future of telecommunications and become part of a culture that thrives on creativity, encourages new ideas, and provides a collaborative and inclusive environment.  

About Us 

As Australia’s specialist fibre and network solutions provider, we own and operate 25,000km of secure, high-capacity fibre connecting people, businesses, governments, and communities across Australia to the world. Through our well-known retail brands, we deliver simple and affordable broadband, mobile, voice and energy services with the purpose of Building Critical Connections. Enabling Better Possibilities.  

Ready to take the next step? 

If you like the sound of this role and think you’d do a great job, but are worried you don’t tick every box, we encourage you to back yourself – we know that diverse groups are less-likely to apply for roles they’re not 100% qualified for, but just as likely to succeed at them!

A Melbourne-based superannuation organisation is seeking an Assurance Analyst - Technology to join their Operations Assurance team on a permanent basis. This is a newly created role focused on strengthening Line 1 (L1) risk and compliance across technology.

You’ll act as the first line of defence, working closely with business and technology teams to ensure controls are operating effectively, risks are understood, and compliance obligations are met.

It’s hands-on, varied, and gives you exposure across cyber, data, resilience, and incident governance - ideal for someone who wants to build real depth in technology risk.

Key Responsibilities:
Perform control testing, assurance activities, and compliance reviews across technology and operations
Support quarterly control self-assessments and ensure obligations are met on time
Act as a first point of contact (SME) for risk and compliance queries from the business
Identify gaps, weaknesses, and risks across systems, processes, and controls
Support business units in addressing internal audit findings and remediation activities
Assist in building and refining assurance methodologies, frameworks, and procedures
Oversee incident governance processes – triage, risk rating, ownership and closure reviews
Provide a bridge between L1 and L2 risk, escalating where required
About You:
Experience in technology risk, IT audit, or controls testing
Strong understanding of GRC / risk & compliance frameworks
Exposure to highly-regulated environments (Superannuation or Financial Services preferred)
Comfortable working in a hands-on, operational role
Strong stakeholder skills - able to support and guide non-risk teams
Certifications such as CISA / CISSP are advantageous
Why This Role
Newly created position with real scope to shape how L1 operates
Broad exposure across technology risk domains
Development pathway into senior risk or L2 roles
Reach out for a confidential conversation with Jodie Marron, Senior Principal Consultant, on 0413 900 039 or jodie@frazertremble.com.au, or apply directly.

Company Description

Nine is Australia’s largest locally owned media company. Working at Nine, you’ll have access to a unique range of experiences and opportunities, helping drive the success of the country’s most trusted television, radio, digital and publishing brands. 

Our content reaches almost every Australian - meaning what we do has real impact. We bring people together, celebrate the big moments, and capture the everyday ones.

Some of our most beloved brands have been part of Australian life for generations, and others - new on the scene - have already found their place firmly in our lives. We’re evolving and we need people like you to bring new ideas, innovate and make your mark.

Job Description

Join Nine as our Workday Cross-Functional Lead, a senior strategic role driving the digital transformation of our People & Culture ecosystem. 

After a successful implementation process, Nine is on the verge of going live with Workday HRIS.  As Workday Cross-Functional Lead, you will lead Nine’s internal Functional Support team.  You will drive the integration and continuous improvement of Workday, translating complex business strategies into scalable, high-impact technology solutions. 

This is a unique opportunity to lead a talented functional team and shape the future of work at Nine during a landmark enterprise-wide evolution.

Day to day you will:

Lead a small team of internal Workday Functional Analysts, overseeing their technical development and prioritising daily support and project delivery.
Drive system governance and platform health, ensuring data integrity, security compliance, and the successful execution of semi-annual Workday releases.
Manage the external AMS provider relationship, acting as the primary liaison for complex Tier 3 escalations and system enhancement specifications.
Maintain the platform roadmap in collaboration with Technology and business leaders to drive relentless process optimisation and automation.
Provide deep functional expertise across Core HCM, Recruitment, Compensation, and Talent modules to resolve escalated configuration challenges.
Provide advice to the Workday Phase 2 implementation team, which will launch Advanced Compensation and Performance modules in July 2026
Qualifications

What you'll bring:

Extensive Workday expertise in a Functional Lead, Architect, or Senior Analyst capacity
Advanced configuration skills across multiple modules, such as Core HCM, Recruitment, Compensation, Talent, and Absence Management.
Proven people leadership capabilities, with a track record of mentoring teams and managing complex workloads effectively.
Strong vendor management experience, specifically in governing external Application Management Services (AMS) and technical partners.
Exceptional communication skills, with the ability to translate intricate technical concepts for non-technical stakeholders and executives.
Analytical rigour and a high attention to detail for managing end-to-end testing, deployment, and system documentation.
Additional Information

How we work

At Nine, our flexible work options vary by role and team. Depending on the position, this may include flexible hours, hybrid work, or part-time arrangements. We welcome discussing your flexibility needs during the hiring process - just ask the Talent Acquisition team. 

Our employee benefits include:

18 weeks paid parental leave with no distinction between primary and secondary carers.
Access to 'Employee Exclusives' program - a way of getting closer to our incredible brands, offering unique experiences, behind-the-scenes access, and awesome perks.
Digital newspaper subscription to our mastheads.
Annual gift voucher for Stan subscription.

More info at Nine Careers.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion:

We're committed to a safe, respectful and inclusive Nine. From day one, you'll be encouraged to bring your whole self to work and will be supported to perform at your best.

We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people with disabilities, and of all ages, genders, nationalities, backgrounds and cultures as we recognise the importance and value of diverse perspectives. Should you require any adjustments to the recruitment process, please advise us when you apply.

Work rights: Please note to apply for this role you must already have the right to lawfully work and live in Australia.

The Company
High growth, ASX Listed Infrastructure business with a strategy for continued growth through organic means and acquisitions. 

This organisation is a clear market leader and key to this success is investing in their people, technology and excellence in delivery for their customers. 

This role is a great entry point into a business that is growing, delivering shareholder value and provides multiple career development pathways.

A rare and influential leadership opportunity has arisen for a highly experienced equities research executive to lead and shape a high‑performing research function across both Australian and International Equities. This role is ideal for a strategic thinker who thrives at the intersection of qualitative insight, quantitative rigour and portfolio construction leadership.
  
The Role
  

Lead the investment research function that blends traditional fundamental analysis with robust quantitative tools and screening methodologies.
Oversee portfolio construction across both Australian and International Equities, ensuring alignment with house philosophy, risk parameters, and investment objectives.
Refine the firm’s portfolio construction philosophy, including frameworks for risk assessment, stress testing, and performance monitoring.
Drive continual improvement in research standards, adopting global best practices and championing the effective use of technology, data, and analytics.
Manage, mentor, and develop the Australian Equity Research team, fostering a collaborative and high‑accountability culture.
Partner with senior investment leaders to influence strategy, optimise processes, and contribute to superior investment outcomes.
  
The Person

Extensive experience applying quantitative frameworks, screening tools, and analytical methods within an equities research or portfolio management environment.
Strong technical understanding of risk modelling, scenario analysis, and stress testing, and how these support portfolio construction and ongoing oversight.
Proven leadership capability with experience managing senior analysts or research teams.
A track record of improving research processes, raising analytical standards, and embedding technology‑driven enhancements.
At least 15 years' experience in equities research, portfolio construction, or related investment leadership roles.
Excellent communication, stakeholder influence, and the ability to operate at executive and Board‑facing levels.
